query: SELECT bio, overseasExperience, funFacts, whyHost, culturalPassions FROM host_listings WHERE hostName = 'Ayi';
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway03.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user JF4SDJ8UQnAgkD3.root --database mkW6ExSEHJcqGWsa6M4fqn --execute SELECT bio, overseasExperience, funFacts, whyHost, culturalPassions FROM host_listings WHERE hostName = 'Ayi';

rows.bio: She’ll be joined by her daughter (management at foreign-owned company) or her daughter’s childhood friend (multinational corporate background), ensuring smooth conversation and cultural bridge-building over the meal or other activities.
rows.overseasExperience: I lived in Singapore for 3 years and traveled extensively across Southeast Asia. I also spent time in Japan learning traditional cooking techniques.
rows.funFacts: I can name every ingredient just by smell! I also collect vintage Chinese teapots and have over 50 in my collection. My neighbors call me the "Dumpling Queen" because I make them every Sunday.
rows.whyHost: After years of traveling and experiencing hospitality around the world, I wanted to share the warmth of Chinese home cooking with visitors. Food has always been my love language, and I believe the best way to understand a culture is through sharing a meal at someone's table. Every guest who leaves my home becomes part of my extended family.
rows.culturalPassions: Beyond food, I'm passionate about traditional Chinese tea ceremonies and calligraphy. I love sharing stories about Shanghai's old neighborhoods and taking guests to hidden local markets that tourists never find. I also practice Tai Chi every morning in the park!

messages: Auntie Lee — a true daughter of Jiading Old Town, the very cradle of Shanghai's famous soup dumplings (Xiao Long Bao). Since 2016, she has been a Superhost on Airbnb, welcoming hundreds of guests into her courtyard home. Though her English is limited, her hospitality is universal: she has traveled widely herself and communicates flawlessly through the language of delicious food.
stdout:
bio	overseasExperience	funFacts	whyHost	culturalPassions
Auntie Lee — a true daughter of Jiading Old Town, the very cradle of Shanghai's famous soup dumplings (Xiao Long Bao). Since 2016, she has been a Superhost on Airbnb, welcoming hundreds of guests into her courtyard home. Though her English is limited, her hospitality is universal: she has traveled widely herself and communicates flawlessly through the language of delicious food.

She’ll be joined by her daughter (management at foreign-owned company) or her daughter’s childhood friend (multinational corporate background), ensuring smooth conversation and cultural bridge-building over the meal or other activities.	I lived in Singapore for 3 years and traveled extensively across Southeast Asia. I also spent time in Japan learning traditional cooking techniques.	I can name every ingredient just by smell! I also collect vintage Chinese teapots and have over 50 in my collection. My neighbors call me the "Dumpling Queen" because I make them every Sunday.	After years of traveling and experiencing hospitality around the world, I wanted to share the warmth of Chinese home cooking with visitors. Food has always been my love language, and I believe the best way to understand a culture is through sharing a meal at someone's table. Every guest who leaves my home becomes part of my extended family.	Beyond food, I'm passionate about traditional Chinese tea ceremonies and calligraphy. I love sharing stories about Shanghai's old neighborhoods and taking guests to hidden local markets that tourists never find. I also practice Tai Chi every morning in the park!
